meeting.title: Q2 Snack Bar Campaign Review
meeting.context: Sarah (Marketing Director/Coach) and Marcus (Digital Marketing Specialist/Coachee) reviewing an organic snack bar campaign

overall.summary: Strong domain integration undermined by a directive, low-safety coaching process. The coach diagnoses and dictates the entire solution, asks only leading questions, interrupts repeatedly, and imposes a deadline despite a stated capacity constraint. Domain/tool relevance is the lone Advanced dimension.

dimensions.name: Powerful Questioning
look_fors_absent: questions that avoid embedding the answer
evidence: Don't you think that's a bit too generic for a niche organic snack bar?
don't you agree that a 30-year-old fitness enthusiast in California buys this bar for a completely different reason than a 30-year-old busy mom in Ohio...?
You know how to write an IF...THEN statement in Tableau... don't you?

dimensions.name: Coachee-Led Problem Solving
evidence: It's easy, you just use the data source tab, drag the CRM table in, and link it via customer email.
Um, yeah, that makes sense. I can look into how to do that join.
coachee's only lead ('I was thinking we could look into behavioral data next...') is immediately taken over by the coach

dimensions.name: Contextual Tool & Domain Relevance
dimensions.note: Content is genuinely Advanced even though delivered through a poor process; rubric #3 measures the integration, not who produces it.
evidence: shouldn't we be using our CRM tags for 'primary benefit' and joining that with our Shopify checkout history? If you do a cross-database join in Tableau, you can isolate the high-frequency buyers from the occasional ones.
create a calculated field... write an IF...THEN statement... to group people into 'Fitness Enthusiasts' versus 'Convenience Buyers'

dimensions.name: Psychological Safety & Active Listening
evidence: opens on the negative: 'our conversion rates are completely flat' with no acknowledgment of the clean dashboards
interrupts 'I was trying to figure out why—'
dismisses a stated roadblock: coachee says 'I haven't blended those specific data sources in Tableau before, but—' and is cut off with 'It's easy...'

dimensions.name: Action-Orientation & Collaborative Accountability
dimensions.note: Has tasks + deadline + success measure, but imposed without verifying capacity or buy-in.
evidence: can you just jump into Tableau right now... email me the updated dashboard link by 9:00 AM tomorrow
capacity signal ignored: 'I have a few other deadlines today, but... yeah, I can get that done.'

participants.name: Sarah
participants.note: Upbeat and confident in tone but directive; does the coachee's thinking for him.

participants.name: Marcus
participants.note: Hesitant, deferential, and under time pressure; repeatedly cut off before completing his reasoning.

recommendations: Replace tag-questions with open diagnostics ('What do you think is driving the flat conversions?') and pause for the coachee to hypothesize.
Flip talk-time: have the coachee propose the segmentation approach and Tableau steps; coach as sounding board.
Validate the coachee's work and treat stated roadblocks as coaching moments before shifting to critique.
Let the coachee set the deadline against real workload and confirm capacity before committing them to downstream timelines.
